subject: Databases
title: CockroachDB
description: A quick, practical guide to CockroachDB.

hook.text: Inspired by Spanner but without Google's atomic clocks. CockroachDB brings distributed SQL to everyone.

buildup.text: CockroachDB uses Raft consensus. Data is split into ranges, each replicated across three or more nodes.

discovery.text: It's PostgreSQL-compatible. Your existing SQL queries and ORMs work with minimal changes.

twist.text: Without atomic clocks, CockroachDB uses hybrid-logical clocks and occasionally waits to ensure ordering.

climax.text: It survives node, rack, and data center failures. 'Cockroach' because it's designed to survive anything.

punchline.text: CockroachDB: distributed SQL with Postgres compatibility. Survives anything.

quiz.question: How is CockroachDB different from Google Spanner?
options: It achieves similar goals without atomic clocks, using hybrid-logical clocks and occasional waits instead
CockroachDB is a NoSQL database while Spanner uses SQL
Spanner is open source and CockroachDB is proprietary
